geni.com
Geoffrey Grisegonelle d'Anjou, Comte d'Anjou, Seneschal of France
French: Geoffroy d'Anjou, Comte d'Anjou, Seneschal of France, Dutch: Godfried van Anjou, Comte d'Anjou, Seneschal of France, German: Gottfried von Anjou, Comte d'Anjou, Seneschal of France
Also Known As:	"known as Grisegonelle ("Greymantle")", "Grisegonelle", "Grisegonnelle", "Of Gatinais /Geoffroi/", "Comté D' Anjou", "Geoffrey "grey gown" d'Anjou", "Greymantle", "Compte D'ANJOU"
Birthdate:	November 11, 940
Birthplace:	Anjou, Pays-de-la-Loire, France
Death:	July 21, 987 (46)
Château-du-Loir, France (Died while besieging MarÃ§on, held by Eudes Ruffin, vassal of Eudes I de Blois) 
Place of Burial:	Châteauneuf (Present Plumereau District of Tours), Anjou (Present département d'Indre-et-Loire), (Present région Pays de la Loire), (Present France)
Immediate Family:	
Son of Fulk II, Count of Anjou and Gerberge du Gâtinais, comtesse d'Anjou

Husband of 
Adélaïde de Chalon, wife of Lambert d'Autun and Geoffroy I d'Anjou and 
Adele de Meaux (de Vermandois)

Father of 
Maurice; 
Ermengarde d'Anjou, Duchess of Bretagne; 
Geoffroy d'Anjou; 
Fulk III "the Black", count of Anjou & Vendôme and 
Gerberge, countess of Anjou

Brother of 
Agnes d'Anjou; Guy d'Anjou; 
Bouchard IV, dit "le Vénérable", III. comte de Vendôme; 
Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou, Reine consort d'Aquitaine; 
Adela d'Anjou; 
seigneur Foulques Ier de Briollay; 
Agnès d'Anjou; 
Aelips d'Anjou; 
'Countess Toulouse' d' Anjou Adelaise Arsinde Blanche;
 Guy d'Anjou, Bishop of Le Puy and Humbert d'Anjou 

Occupation:	Count of Anjou vv.960 -.987, Count of Anjou, Anjoun Kreivi 960, count of anjou, graaf van Anjou, Count of Soissons



“Royal Ancestry: A Study in Colonial & Medieval Families,” Douglas Richardson (2013):
  “ADÈLE OF TROYES, married about 965 GEOFFROI I GRISEGONELLE, Count of Anjou, 958/960-987, Count of Châlon, 979-987, son of Foulques II the Good, Count of Anjou, by his wife, Gerberge. They had two sons, Foulques III Nerra [Count of Anjou] and Geoffroi, and two daughters, Ermengarde and Gerberge (wife of Guillaume IV, Count of Angoulême). His wife, Adèle, was living 6 March 974. He married (2nd) 20 or 9 March 979 ADÈLE ___, widow of Lambert I, Count of Chalon-sur-Saône (died 22 Feb. 978). They had one son, Maurice. GEOFFROI I, Count of Anjou, was slain in battle at Marcon (near Château-du-Loir) 21 July 987, and was buried at Saint-Martin de Tours. His widow, Adèle, was living in 999. 
  Anselme Hist. de la Maison Royale de France 6 (1730): 3-21 (sub Anjou). Étienne-Gallois La Champagne et les derniers Carlovingiens (1853): 139-145. Lot Les derniers Carolingiens: Lothaire, Louis V., Charles de Lorraine (954-991) (1891): 323-331. Métais Cartulaire Saintongeais de la Trinité de Vendôme (Archives historiques de la Saintonge et de l’Aunis 22) (1893): 2-11. Cartulaire de l’Abbaye cardinals de la Dinite de Vendôme 1 (1893): 10-11 (Genealogia Comitum Andegavensium). René Poupardin, "Généalogies angevines du XIe Siècle," in Mélanges d'Archéologie et d'Histoire 20 (1900): 199-208. Halphen Recueil d'Annales Angevines et Vendômoises (1903). Halphen & Poupardin Chroniques des Comtes d'Anjou et des Seigneurs d'Amboise (1913): 247-250 (Genealogiæ Comitum Andegavensium). Schwennicke Europäische Stammtafeln 3 (1984): 49 (sub Vermandois), 116 (sub Anjou). "Notes sur quelques Vicomtes de Châtellerault" in Bull. de la Société des Antiquaires de l'Ouest 16 (1981): 261-270. Bernard S. Bachrach, "Geoffrey Greymantle, count of the Angevins, 960-987: a Study in French Politics," Studies in Medieval & Renaissance Hist. n.s. 7 (1985): 3-65. Winter Descs. of Charlemagne (800-1400) (1987): VIII.2, IX.2-IX.4. Bachrach Falk Nerra (1993). Marchegay & Salmon Chroniques d'Anjou (1856). Marchegay & Mabille Chroniques des Églises d'Anjou (1869): 164-165 (Chronicon Vindocinense seu de Aquaria sub A.D. 1187: "Obiit Gosfredus, Andecavorum comes, pater Fulconis ultimi, XII kalendas augusti [21 July], in obsidione Marsonis super Odonem Rufinum facta."). Bouchard Sword, Miter, & Cloister (1987): 307-309. Kerrebrouck La Préhistoire des Capetians (1993). Bouchard Those of My Blood (2001): 19 (chart), 149-154.”

!MARRIAGE: We Veitches, Veacthes, Veaches, Veeches, Vol II pg. 6 Lineage #5,  person 34 (book in possession of Sam Coverston, Bushnell FL).

MALE Geoffroy I "Grisegonelle", d. 987, count of Anjou. In a charter of 970, count Gaufridus mentions his father Fulco and mother Gerberga [Cart. S.-Aubin 1: 38-9].


He married Adele of Meaux (934-982), daughter of Robert of Vermandois  and Adelais de Vergy. Their children were:

   1. Fulk III of Anjou.
   2. Ermengarde of Anjou (b. 965), married Conan I of Rennes.
   3. Gerberga (b. 973), married Count William IV of Angoulême.

He married, secondly, to Adelaise de Chalon in Mar 979 and had one child:

   1. Maurice of Anjou (980 - 1012), married to a daughter of Aimery, Count of Saintes and had one son.

Geoffrey I of Anjou (died July 21, 987), known as Grisegonelle ("Greymantle"), was count of Anjou from 960 to 987. He succeeded his father Fulk II. He cultivated the loyal support of a group of magnates, some of whom he inherited from his father,  others whom he recruited: men such as Alberic of Vihiers, Cadilo of Blaison, Roger I (le "vieux") of Loudon, Joscelin of Rennes, castellan of Baugé, Suhard I of Craon, Tobert of Buzençais and members of the Bouchard clan, and encouraged them to see their own dynastic interests as tied to the success of the Angevin count.  He succeeded in establishing a group of fideles upon whom his son, Fulk called "Nerra", was able to depend in establishing Anjou as a cohesive regional power in an age of territorial disintegration. In preparing the way, Geoffrey was the first count in the west of France to associate his son in the comital title.

Geoffrey allied with the Count of Nantes against the Count of Rennes, and allied with Hugh Capet, fearing an invasion by the Count of Blois. He was one of the men responsible for bringing Hugh to the throne of France.

Geoffrey I of Anjou (died July 21, 987), known as Grisegonelle ("Greymantle"), was count of Anjou from 960 to 987. He succeeded his father Fulk II. He cultivated the loyal support of a group of magnates, some of whom he inherited from his father,  others whom he recruited: men such as Alberic of Vihiers, Cadilo of Blaison, Roger I (le "vieux") of Loudon, Joscelin of Rennes, castellan of Baugé, Suhard I of Craon, Tobert of Buzençais and members of the Bouchard clan, and encouraged them to see their own dynastic interests as tied to the success of the Angevin count.  He succeeded in establishing a group of fideles upon whom his son, Fulk called "Nerra", was able to depend in establishing Anjou as a cohesive regional power in an age of territorial disintegration. In preparing the way, Geoffrey was the first count in the west of France to associate his son in the comital title.

britannica
First dynasty of counts
Under one of the sons of Robert the Strong, Anjou was entrusted to a certain Ingelger, who became the founder of the first Angevin dynasty. Ingelger’s son Fulk I the Red rid the country of the Normans and enlarged his domains by taking part of Touraine. He died in 942, and under his successor, Fulk II the Good, the destruction caused by the preceding wars was repaired. Geoffrey I Grisegonelle, who succeeded Fulk II in about 960, began the policy of expansion that was to characterize this first feudal dynasty. He helped Hugh Capet to seize the French crown but died some months after the new king’s accession (987).

Geoffrey’s successor Fulk III Nerra, one of the most remarkable figures of his period and the most powerful member of the dynasty, ruled from 987 to 1040. He finally drove his encroaching neighbours back beyond the frontiers of Anjou and built strongly fortified castles along the border of his territory. Fulk’s son Geoffrey II Martel (1040–60) pursued the policy of expansion begun by his father and annexed the Vendômois and a part of Maine to Anjou. Because he left no sons, his two nephews, Geoffrey III the Bearded and Fulk IV le Réchin, shared the succession. However, they soon came into armed conflict, and Fulk defeated Geoffrey in 1068. Nevertheless, he had to give up most of the lands that Fulk III Nerra had acquired and to defend his fief against the claims of the Duke of Normandy. After the death of Fulk IV in 1109, his son Fulk V the Young endeavoured to make good the losses caused by the various wars. He married his son Geoffrey Plantagenet to Matilda, the daughter of Henry I of England and widow of the emperor Henry V.

By his marriage to Matilda, Geoffrey IV Plantagenet acquired a claim to Normandy and England. Forced to spend his whole life fighting his rivals and the Angevin castellans, he nevertheless succeeded in pacifying Anjou, which in 1151 he left to his son Henry (later Henry II of England), Count of Anjou and Maine and Duke of Normandy, who married Eleanor of Aquitaine after the annulment of her marriage to Louis VII of France. Thus the Anglo-Angevin empire of the Plantagenet dynasty was founded, extending from England to the Pyrenees.

Philip II Augustus of France, however, conquered Anjou from John of England at the beginning of the 13th century. An attempt by the English to retake Anjou failed when they were defeated at La Roche-aux-Moines in 1214. Anjou was definitively ceded to France by the Treaty of Paris (1259)

zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffroy Ier 'Grisegonelle' comte d'Anjou於0938年11月11日出生在法國o-阿爾培斯易瑟艾安喬時，他的父親Foulques II d'Anjou29歲，母親Gerberge de Gâtinais25歲。他於0959年3月2日與Adèle de Vermandois comtesse d'Anjou在法國豪-德-福朗斯艾斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於0987年7月21日在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易薩特a奧-度-洛易享年48歲，埋葬在法國崔英德-吉洛易圖爾斯。

nameMeanings.name: Geoffroy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Yiddish ber ‘bear’, probably influenced by the early medieval European practice of giving animal names to people. It is often paired with Dov in order to provide a Hebrew name in certain rituals.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffroy Ier 'Grisegonelle' comte d'Anjou was born on 11 November 0938, in Anjou, Isère, Rhône-Alpes, France, his father, Foulques II d'Anjou, was 29 and his mother, Gerberge de Gâtinais, was 25. He married Adèle de Vermandois comtesse d'Anjou on 2 March 0959, in Aisne, Hauts-de-France, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 21 July 0987, in Château-du-Loir, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France, at the age of 48, and was buried in Tours, Indre-et-Loire, Centre, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffroy Ier 'Grisegonelle' comte d'Anjou est né le 11 novembre 0938, à Anjou, Isère, Rhône-Alpes, France, son père, Foulques II d'Anjou, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Gerberge de Gâtinais, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Adèle de Vermandois comtesse d'Anjou le 2 mars 0959, à Aisne, Hauts-de-France, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 21 juillet 0987, à Château-du-Loir, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterré à Tours, Indre-et-Loire, Centre, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffroy Ier 'Grisegonelle' comte d'Anjou am 11. November 0938 in Anjou, Isère, Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Foulques II d'Anjou 29 und seine Mutter Gerberge de Gâtinais 25. Er hat am 2. März 0959 in Aisne, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich Adèle de Vermandois comtesse d'Anjou geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 21. Juli 0987 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Château-du-Loir, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich und wurde in Tours, Indre-et-Loire, Centre, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffroy Ier 'Grisegonelle' comte d'Anjou è nato il il 11 novembre 0938 nel Anjou, Isère, Rhône-Alpes, Francia, il suo padre, Foulques II d'Anjou, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Gerberge de Gâtinais, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Adèle de Vermandois comtesse d'Anjou il 2 marzo 0959, nel Aisne, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 21 luglio 0987, nel Château-du-Loir, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Francia, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolto a Tours, Indre-et-Loire, Centre, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Geoffroy Ier 'Grisegonelle' comte d'Anjouが0938年11月11日にフランスルhoネ-アルペスイスeアアンジョウで生まれた時，父Foulques II d'Anjouは29歳，母Gerberge de Gâtinaisは25歳であった。0959年3月2日にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスエイスンでAdèle de Vermandois comtesse d'Anjouと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は0987年7月21日にフランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーサルスチaテアウ-ドウ-ロイルで48歳で死去し，フランスセントアインドレ-エト-ロイアートウルズにて埋葬された。

ko.Château-du-Loir, Sarthe, Pays de la Loir: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 cha테오-드-로이르

ko.Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르
ko.lifeSummary: Geoffroy Ier 'Grisegonelle' comte d'Anjou(이)가 0938년 11월 11일 프랑스 ho니-알프스 이스e르 앤조우에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Foulques II d'Anjou(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Gerberge de Gâtinais(은)는 25세였다. 그는 0959년 3월 2일 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 아이스니에서 Adèle de Vermandois comtesse d'Anjou(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 0987년 7월 21일 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 cha테오-드-로이르에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 세트르 인드레-트-로이르 토우르스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffroy Ier 'Grisegonelle' comte d'Anjou nasceu em 11 de novembro de 0938, em Anjou, Isère, Rhône-Alpes, França, seu pai, Foulques II d'Anjou, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Gerberge de Gâtinais, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Adèle de Vermandois comtesse d'Anjou em 2 de março de 0959, em Aisne, Hauts-de-France, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 21 de julho de 0987, em Château-du-Loir, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, França, com 48 anos, e foi sepultado em Tours, Indre-et-Loire, Centre, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffroy Ier 'Grisegonelle' comte d'Anjou родился 11 ноября 0938 г. в местности анджоу, исeр, рoне-алпесь, Франция, его отцу по имени Foulques II d'Anjou было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Gerberge de Gâtinais, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Adèle de Vermandois comtesse d'Anjou 2 марта 0959 г. в местности эисн, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 21 июля 0987 г. в местности чaто-ду-лоир, сарт, пеис де ла лоир, Франция в возрасте 48 лет и был похоронен в местности тоурс, индре-ет-лоир, кентр, Франция.
